rationale: 7.8-year Search Engineer at product companies (Nykaa, Vedantu, Observe.AI) who shipped sentence-transformers + FAISS semantic search (bge-base), XGBoost/LightGBM LTR ranking models, and led a keyword-to-embedding migration — directly satisfying all core IR and ranking requirements; minor gap is no explicit NDCG/evaluation-framework ownership cited.
Graphic Designer at fictional honeypot company Wayne Enterprises with a marketing/customer-support background and zero ML, NLP, search, or retrieval engineering work of any kind.

Only 2.2 years of experience entirely at IT-services firms (HCL, Cognizant) doing test automation and DevOps; AI is self-described as self-learner level, and listed ML skills (Object Detection, Image Classification) are CV-focused with no production NLP, IR, or ranking work.

10.6-year career entirely in non-technical roles (HR Manager, Marketing Manager, Project Manager, Customer Support) at fictional Pied Piper and Wayne Enterprises; no engineering, ML, or IR work whatsoever.
HR Manager career at Infosys (IT services) and fictional Wayne Enterprises with actual work in accounting, business analysis, and marketing; no ML or engineering depth despite a B.Tech EE background.

11-year career in Sales, HR, and Marketing across TCS (IT services) and fictional Initech with no engineering or ML production work; skills are beginner-level non-ML tools (Excel, Terraform, Snowflake) with no evidence of any retrieval or ranking work.

DevOps Engineer at product company Swiggy with advanced Qdrant (32 months) and LlamaIndex in skills indicating meaningful vector-DB exposure, but the sole career description is cloud infrastructure (AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes) and the summary explicitly frames ML/AI as self-learner level with a side project — no production IR or ranking system ownership demonstrated.

Junior ML Engineer at upGrad (product company) with tier_1 IISc education, advanced Information Retrieval (39 months), intermediate Milvus (35 months), and advanced PEFT (40 months) skills, but only 3 years experience and the sole career description is time-series supply-chain forecasting — no production search or ranking system shipped.

6.2-year full-stack/DevOps engineer with a stint at Accenture consulting and fictional Dunder Mifflin; top ML skill is Diffusion Models (generative/CV), and AI exposure is self-learner level — no production NLP, retrieval, or ranking work in any role.

10-year career entirely in non-technical roles (Business Analyst, HR Manager, Operations Manager, Civil Engineer) at fictional Stark Industries and Wayne Enterprises plus TCS; no ML, AI, or software engineering work despite a Chemical Engineering PhD.
